name: Khadija bint Ali
title: Patient Lady of Hashem
role: Sister of the Master of Martyrs
shortDescription: The daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was present in Karbala and endured the sufferings of captivity along with the other women of the Ahl al-Bayt.
fullStory:
Khadija bint Ali was the daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and another brave and patient sister of Imam Hussain (AS). She grew up in a house full of knowledge, faith, and bravery.

When Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan moved from Medina to Mecca and then Karbala, Khadija was also with her brother and embraced all the hardships of this long journey. On the day of Ashura, she witnessed the unjust martyrdom of her brothers, nephews, and the Imam's loyal companions.

After the looting of the tents, she, along with her sisters Lady Zaynab (SA) and Umm Kulthum (SA), was taken captive by the ruthless Kufan army. On the long route to Kufa and Sham, despite immense grief, Khadija nursed the children and orphans with patience and perseverance.

After returning to Medina, she mourned the Master of Martyrs (AS) and the other martyrs of Karbala until the end of her life, becoming a narrator of the Ahl al-Bayt's (AS) oppression for future generations.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَيْكُنَّ يا بَناتِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ...
quote: (In her eulogy:) Peace be upon you, O daughters of the Messenger of God, who endured the suffering of captivity.
